title: Per-plugin workers unstartable: worker_runtime invoked as unresolvable module
description: While building a test plugin to validate the lifecycle, found the same class of bug just fixed for the daemon itself (daz-python-bridge-wn3), but for every per-plugin venv: WorkerManager spawns '<plugin_venv_python> -m daemon.worker_runtime --entry ...', but PluginDependencyInstaller (C++, sop.4) never installs the daemon package into a plugin's own venv -- only requirements.txt, if present. Every plugin worker would crash with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'daemon' on its very first start.
design: worker_runtime.py has zero third-party dependencies (stdlib only: argparse, importlib.util, json, sys), so the fix is to invoke it by direct file path instead of as a package module -- no need to install anything extra into per-plugin venvs at all (which would otherwise needlessly drag fastapi/uvicorn into every plugin). daemon/app.py's _build_command now resolves _WORKER_RUNTIME_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / 'worker_runtime.py' and spawns [python, str(_WORKER_RUNTIME_PATH), --entry, ...]. Added a regression test asserting the real _build_command (not the test fixture's override, which already worked around this by coincidence) never uses -m daemon.worker_runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Verified against a real manually-installed plugin (examples/hello_plugin) and a real daemon process: start -> running, stop -> ready, restart -> running (new pid), disable -> disabled, start-while-disabled -> 409, enable -> ready, start -> running. Full lifecycle confirmed working end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed in daemon/app.py: worker_runtime.py invoked by direct file path instead of -m daemon.worker_runtime. Regression test added (test_build_command_invokes_worker_runtime_by_direct_path). Verified end-to-end with a real plugin through the full start/stop/restart/enable/disable lifecycle against a real daemon process.

title: daemon package unimportable: bundle daemon/ source via resources/BlueMoonFoundry convention
description: Live testing (after daz-python-bridge-5l4 wired up daemon launch) showed the daemon subprocess crashing immediately: 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named daemon'. DaemonProcess launches 'python -m uvicorn daemon.app:app' with cwd=DaemonPaths::baseDir() (the writable per-user AppData tree, which only ever holds generated state -- run_venv, per-plugin venvs), but nothing ever put the daemon/ Python source anywhere on disk for the launched process to import.
design: Matches the user's established BlueMoonFoundry convention (confirmed via the ContentBrowser plugin's resources/BlueMoonFoundry/ContentBrowser/ layout): build.sh install now copies daemon/ + pyproject.toml into <DAZ_STUDIO_EXE_DIR>/resources/BlueMoonFoundry/DazPythonBridge/, sibling to plugins/. UvBootstrapper gained setDaemonSourceDir(), and its install-deps step now runs 'uv pip install --reinstall <daemonSourceDir>' instead of hand-naming fastapi/uvicorn -- this installs the actual daemon/ package (pulling in its pyproject.toml-declared deps) as a real site-packages entry, so daemon.app:app is importable regardless of the subprocess's cwd. DzPythonBridgePane resolves the bundled path at runtime via dzApp->getResourcesPath() (SDK-provided, present in both SDK4/SDK6 headers) and passes it in before calling ensureReady() -- UvBootstrapper itself stays DAZ-SDK-independent, taking the resolved path as a plain string.
acceptance_criteria: Verified directly (without needing a live DAZ Studio session) by running the exact uv pip install command against the real bootstrapped run_venv, then launching uvicorn daemon.app:app from an unrelated cwd and curling /health successfully.
close_reason: build.sh install bundles daemon/+pyproject.toml to resources/BlueMoonFoundry/DazPythonBridge/; UvBootstrapper's install-deps step uv-pip-installs from there instead of naming fastapi/uvicorn by hand; DzPythonBridgePane resolves the path via dzApp->getResourcesPath(). Verified end-to-end: uv pip install succeeded, daemon.app:app imports from an unrelated cwd, uvicorn serves /health successfully. All C++ harnesses and pytest suite still pass.

title: Pane: mojibake in PID/Memory placeholder text; last_used inaccurate/discarded on stop
description: Two bugs found via manual lifecycle testing of the pane's Installed Plugins table: (1) PID/Memory columns showed garbled text instead of an em dash placeholder -- QString::fromLatin1("-") on a UTF-8-encoded em dash literal decodes each of its 3 UTF-8 bytes as a separate Latin-1 character (mojibake), introduced by the earlier blanket QStringLiteral->fromLatin1 conversion for Qt4 compat (7wq) which didn't account for non-ASCII literals. (2) last_used showed 'never' after stopping a plugin that had clearly just been used -- WorkerManager discarded last_used entirely when the _Worker object was torn down in stop(); separately, even while a worker was alive, last_used was sent as a raw time.monotonic() reading rather than an elapsed-seconds value, which the client already displays as "%1s ago" -- a real number there would have shown something like '257530s ago', not a sensible time.
design: Fix 1: replaced the two QString::fromLatin1(em-dash) call sites with QString(QChar(0x2014)), sidestepping source-encoding-vs-decoder mismatches entirely. Verified no other non-ASCII fromLatin1() literals exist anywhere else in src/include. Fix 2: WorkerManager gained a self._last_used dict that survives stop()/crash/eviction, updated at spawn and at each call; status() now returns time.monotonic() - last_used (elapsed seconds) instead of the raw timestamp, computed server-side so the client needs no clock reconciliation of its own.
acceptance_criteria: Verified via curl against a real daemon + hello_plugin: start (last_used~0.06s), status while running (~2.1s), stop (~2.2s, persisted not null), all small sensible elapsed-seconds values. Added 3 new WorkerManager pytest cases covering elapsed-seconds semantics, persistence across stop, and null-for-never-started. Full suite (53 tests) passes.
close_reason: Both fixed and verified end-to-end against a real daemon process. Em-dash mojibake fixed via QChar(0x2014); last_used now persists across stop and reports server-computed elapsed seconds instead of a raw monotonic timestamp. Rebuilt and reinstalled against both real SDK4 and SDK6 DAZ Studio installs.

title: Wire daemon bootstrap/launch into the pane; add status + log display
description: The pane never actually triggered UvBootstrapper::ensureReady() or DaemonProcess::start() -- sop.1 explicitly deferred that wiring to wby, whose own scope turned out to only cover pane shell registration, not the bootstrap trigger. Result: opening the pane showed an empty plugin table and clicking Start/Stop did nothing, with no error surfaced anywhere, because the daemon was never launched at all.
design: DzPythonBridgePane now owns UvBootstrapper, DaemonProcess, and DaemonHealthMonitor instances, wired in sequence: ensureReady() -> on ready() call DaemonProcess::start() -> DaemonHealthMonitor polls independently and is the authoritative up/down signal. Added a color-coded one-line daemon status label and a read-only log view (DSS-style [HH:mm:ss] [SOURCE] message lines) fed by bootstrap step signals, daemon process started/crashed events, and a new DaemonProcess::logLine(QString) signal that streams the daemon subprocess's own stdout/stderr line-by-line (previously silently discarded).
acceptance_criteria: Opening the pane visibly bootstraps and launches the daemon with status feedback (bootstrapping/starting/running/unreachable/failed) and a scrolling log of what's happening, verified by building and installing against both real SDK4 and SDK6 DAZ Studio installs on this machine.
close_reason: Wired UvBootstrapper -> DaemonProcess -> DaemonHealthMonitor into DzPythonBridgePane; added daemon status label + log view; added DaemonProcess::logLine() streaming the daemon subprocess's stdout/stderr. Built and installed clean against both real SDK4 and SDK6 DAZ Studio installs; all C++ harnesses and pytest suite still pass.

title: DPB: zip extraction hardening for plugin installs
description: Harden the drag-and-drop plugin zip installer against path traversal (zip-slip), symlink entries, and zip-bomb resource exhaustion, since archives are effectively untrusted input dropped by the user. Originally flagged as a spec gap with no defenses specified.
design: Vendor miniz (single-header, MIT) matching the project existing cpp-httplib vendoring style -- no QuaZip or libarchive. Reject any entry with an absolute path, a double-dot segment, or a symlink attribute. Enforce max total uncompressed size, max entry count, and per-entry compression-ratio caps, checked live during decompression, not just trusted from header fields, so a crafted archive cannot lie about its size. Extract to a staging dir, validate manifest.json there, and only atomically rename into the final plugin dir once fully validated -- the live plugins dir never contains a partially-extracted or rejected archive. Note: this defends the filesystem, not against a malicious plugin actual Python code -- arbitrary code execution is inherent to running a third-party plugin and is a separate trust-model concern for community distribution, not solved by extraction hardening.
acceptance_criteria: Archives with path-traversal entries, symlink entries, or oversized/bomb content are rejected before or during extraction with no writes outside the staging dir; valid archives land atomically in the final plugin dir.
close_reason: ZipInstaller (miniz-backed, staged+atomic-rename) implemented and verified against 5 crafted zip fixtures (path traversal, symlink, oversized entry, and a lying-header zip bomb proving live byte-count enforcement) via a standalone C++ test harness, 17/17 assertions passing. Deliberately decoupled from token auth (sop.7) it's nominally blocked by, since extraction hardening doesn't depend on HTTP auth wiring.

title: No HTTP route for PluginRegistry.call() -- exposed plugin functions unreachable
description: PluginRegistry.call() (routes resolving/disabled checks before handing off to WorkerManager.call()) exists and is tested, but daemon/app.py never exposes it as a FastAPI route. Discovered while manually validating a test plugin's lifecycle: start/stop/restart/enable/disable all work over HTTP, but there's no way to actually invoke one of a running plugin's EXPOSED_FUNCTIONS (e.g. examples/hello_plugin's hello()/add()/boom()) without writing to the worker's stdin directly. Likely needs something like POST /plugins/{id}/call with {function, args, kwargs} in the body, mirroring the shape WorkerManager.call() already expects.
notes: Not implemented -- filed for future work, out of scope for the lifecycle validation session that found it.

title: DPB: DAZ Studio plugin pane shell for bridge management
description: The epic has a task (.6) for the plugin status/management UI *content* (state list, start/stop/restart/enable/disable controls, daemon polling logic) but no task for the DAZ Studio-side plugin registration itself: a dockable DzViewportPane-derived pane class, its DSS plugin/menu registration (Window > Panes entry), and the base Qt widget/layout shell that .6's polling and controls attach to. Without this the .6 work has no host surface to live in.
design: Follows DSS's existing pane plugin conventions (DzViewportPane subclass or equivalent dockable pane API), registered via the DSS plugin manager so it appears under Window > Panes as 'Daz Python Bridge'. Provides the empty widget shell (layout, list view stub) that .6 populates with live plugin state.
acceptance_criteria: A new dockable pane titled 'Daz Python Bridge' is registered and appears in DSS's Window > Panes menu; opening/closing/docking it behaves like other DSS panes; the pane exposes a container widget that .6's status list and controls can be mounted into.
close_reason: Pane shell + plugin registration implemented and verified (real configure+build against SDK4/Qt4, SDK6/Qt6, and standalone Core)

title: DPB: token authentication reusing existing SecureRandom
description: Secure the daemon port 18812 endpoints with a token, matching the existing DSS auth model on port 18811, without reimplementing token generation or crypto in Python.
design: DSS remains the sole token generator: reuse AuthenticationService and SecureRandom generateHexToken exactly as done for the existing dazscriptserver_token.txt, writing to a new dazpythonbridge_token.txt under the same daz3d dir with the same chmod-600 enforcement and warning. The Python daemon only ever reads this file at its own startup via an X-DPB-Token header check -- it never generates or rotates a token itself. Single source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: DPB daemon rejects requests without a valid X-DPB-Token; token file is generated by DSS existing SecureRandom-backed code path, not by any Python-side crypto.
close_reason: Ported SecureRandom/AuthenticationService from daz-script-server for dazpythonbridge_token.txt; daemon enforces X-DPB-Token on /plugins* via daemon/auth.py, DSS outbound callers attach it

title: DPB: plugin status/management UI and daemon API
description: Non-modal plugin status pane in DSS showing each installed plugin state, resolving, ready, starting, running, failed, or disabled, with start, stop, restart, enable, and disable controls, so users have visibility into background installs and worker health without anything blocking the UI.
design: Daemon exposes GET /plugins, returning state, pid, memory, last-used, and POST /plugins/ID/start, stop, restart, enable, disable. DSS polls via QTimer using async QNetworkAccessManager calls, never a blocking call on the Qt main thread, consistent with the existing AsyncRequestManager polling pattern already used for async DazScript requests. A plugin in resolving or disabled state rejects calls to its exposed_functions with a distinct DazPluginNotReadyError rather than a raw connection failure.
acceptance_criteria: Status pane reflects install progress, running, idle, and failed state, and responds to manual start, stop, restart, enable, disable without freezing the DAZ Studio UI at any point.
close_reason: Implemented plugin registry + GET/POST /plugins daemon API, PluginStatusManager async poller, and DzPythonBridgePane status table/controls

title: DPB: warm worker process lifecycle and crash policy
description: Each plugin isolated venv needs its own live Python process to actually execute calls, since a venv is not a process. Spawn a persistent warm worker subprocess per plugin lazily on first call, not eagerly, and not spawn-per-call which would pay interpreter startup latency on every invocation.
design: Worker spawned on first /execute call routed to that plugin_id, using that plugin own venv interpreter, communicating over a local pipe or loopback socket. Idle-timeout eviction, for example 10 minutes unused, reclaims memory. Crash policy: one automatic restart on worker crash; if it crashes again, flip to failed state and require manual restart via the status UI -- unlimited silent auto-restart would crash-loop a systematically broken plugin invisibly.
acceptance_criteria: First call to a plugin has a visible but bounded cold start; repeated calls are fast with no re-spawn; an idle plugin worker is reaped after timeout and respawns transparently on next call; a worker that crashes twice in a row ends in failed state, not a restart loop.
close_reason: WorkerManager + worker_runtime implemented with lazy spawn, idle eviction, and restart-once-then-fail crash policy, verified by pytest (7/7 passing) using a real subprocess protocol. Deliberately decoupled from venv/zip layout (sop.3/.4) via an injected build_command callback so it isn't blocked on those landing first; sop.6 will wire it into the FastAPI /plugins endpoints.

title: DPB: per-plugin venv isolation and eager dependency resolution
description: Each installed plugin gets its own venv instead of a shared site-packages, so two plugins pinning conflicting dependency versions do not clobber each other. Dependencies are resolved eagerly at install time, right after the zip is committed, not lazily on first call, so a broken requirements.txt surfaces immediately in the plugin status pane instead of on first invocation.
design: uv venv for the plugin dir, then uv pip install -r requirements.txt scoped to that venv. Reuses the Python 3.11 build already downloaded during bootstrap so per-plugin venv creation is sub-second. Disk cost stays low across many plugins because uv global package cache is content-addressable and hardlinks shared packages such as numpy across venvs rather than duplicating them. This replaces the original spec single shared venv with a --target install approach.
acceptance_criteria: Two plugins with conflicting dependency versions install and run without interfering; a plugin with an unresolvable requirements.txt fails at install time, status failed, rather than at first call.
close_reason: Per-plugin venv isolation + eager dependency resolution implemented via PluginDependencyInstaller/PluginInstaller; verified conflicting-version coexistence and eager install-time failure with a real uv.

title: DPB: /run inline Python execution endpoint + scripting pane
description: Add POST /run to the daemon: executes inline Python in the isolated run_venv, captures print() output as log lines plus a single JSON-serializable return object, mirroring how DazScript inline /execute already works in the other direction. Add a DSS UI pane, Script-IDE style, where users type Python and click Execute.
design: run_venv stays fully isolated -- it must NOT import already-installed plugin packages, keeping it a clean-slate scratch environment like DAZ Studio own Script IDE.
acceptance_criteria: User can type Python in the new pane, execute it, see print() output and the returned value; run_venv has no visibility into plugin venvs.
close_reason: POST /run + inline_runner/inline_runtime + InlineRunner + pane Script-IDE section implemented and tested

title: DPB: daemon bootstrap and JIT venv setup
description: First-run JIT bootstrap for the DPB daemon: download platform uv binary, install Python 3.11 via uv, create the isolated run_venv (used only by the /run inline-scripting endpoint, not by plugins), and launch the FastAPI daemon bound to 127.0.0.1:18812. Everything else in the DPB epic depends on this daemon existing and being launchable/health-checkable from DSS.
design: Layout: AppData/Daz 3D/Studio4/DazPythonBridge/bin/uv, run_venv/, plugins/ID/venv/. Per-plugin venvs are created lazily elsewhere, NOT at bootstrap time -- bootstrap only creates run_venv/. DSS must be able to detect daemon liveness and know if the daemon process died so the plugin status UI can reflect it.
acceptance_criteria: uv and Python 3.11 install triggers on first pane access if missing; run_venv created and isolated from any plugin venv; daemon launches on 18812, bound to loopback only; DSS can detect daemon up/down state.
notes: Implemented and build-verified. DaemonPaths/UvBootstrapper/DaemonProcess/DaemonHealthMonitor (Qt-only core lib, no DAZ SDK dep) built clean against Qt6 devkit at Y:/working/BlueMoonFoundry/qt-devkit/6.10.3/msvc2022_64 and DAZ SDK6 win32 at Y:/working/BlueMoonFoundry/daz-studio-sdks/daz-studio-6-sdk-win32. Python daemon (FastAPI /health) runtime-verified with uv/uvicorn. Caught and fixed a real bug: CMAKE_AUTOMOC_MOC_OPTIONS -i copied from daz-script-server's CMakeLists breaks MOC for header-declared QObject classes -- that flag is only valid for classes mocked inline via #include "foo.moc" in a .cpp, not for the general case. 'Triggers on first pane access' wiring is deferred to daz-python-bridge-wby (pane shell), which will call UvBootstrapper::ensureReady().
close_reason: Daemon bootstrap/JIT venv backend implemented and build-verified against Qt6 devkit + DAZ SDK6; Python daemon runtime-verified.

title: Daz Python Bridge (DPB): bidirectional Python plugin ecosystem
description: Add a Python daemon (port 18812) that DazScript can call into synchronously, plus a drag-and-drop plugin installer, so DazScript-driven workflows can invoke Python (numpy/ML/HTTP clients) inline and the community can distribute installable Python add-ons for DSS. Complements the existing dazpy client (Python calling INTO DAZ via port 18811) with the reverse direction (DAZ calling INTO Python). Full design worked out gap-by-gap in conversation before implementation; see child issues for the resolved architecture per gap.
design: Two ports: 18811 (existing, DAZ-as-server) unchanged. 18812 (new) is a FastAPI daemon DSS launches. Two venv kinds: an isolated run_venv/ for the inline /run scripting pane (Script-IDE analogue), and one isolated venv per installed plugin under plugins/<id>/venv/ (solves shared-site-packages dependency conflicts). Each plugin gets a lazily-spawned warm worker subprocess (not spawn-per-call) with idle-timeout eviction and a one-shot auto-restart-then-fail-loud crash policy. Zip installs are hardened (miniz, path-traversal/symlink rejection, live-enforced size caps, stage-then-commit atomic install) and dependency-resolved eagerly at install time so failures surface immediately in a non-modal plugin status pane (states: resolving/ready/starting/running/failed/disabled) with start/stop/restart/enable/disable controls. DSS's outbound calls to the daemon use QNetworkAccessManager (async) + QTimer polling, never blocking the Qt main thread. Token auth reuses the existing SecureRandom/AuthenticationService C++ code (DSS generates+owns the token file, daemon only reads it) rather than reimplementing crypto in Python. Reinstall follows the existing script-registry convention (overwrite-by-id), sequenced as stop-worker -> stage-commit -> re-resolve-deps.
acceptance_criteria: All 9 originally-identified spec gaps have a resolved design reflected in child issues; child issues are implementable independently once their listed dependencies are closed.
close_reason: All 9 child issues closed: daemon bootstrap, /run scripting pane, hardened zip installs, per-plugin venv isolation, warm worker lifecycle, plugin status UI, token auth, plugin pane shell, and reinstall/overwrite-by-id semantics.

title: Pane: disable plugin action buttons with no selection; add Installed Plugins section header
description: Two related clarity issues in the pane: (1) Start/Stop/Restart/Enable/Disable were always enabled even with no plugin row selected (or no plugins installed at all), so clicking them silently no-op'd; (2) the plugin table/buttons had no section header, sitting directly below the daemon status/log block with nothing distinguishing 'these buttons act on a plugin' from 'this text describes the daemon'.
close_reason: Buttons now start disabled and track table selection via itemSelectionChanged()/updateActionButtonsEnabled(), also re-evaluated after every plugin list refresh. Added an 'Installed Plugins' section header (matching the existing 'Run Python' section's own header pattern) plus per-button tooltips clarifying they act on the selected row, not the daemon. Verified: builds and installs clean against both real SDK4 and SDK6 DAZ Studio installs; pytest suite unaffected.

title: Slow down plugin-list/health polling from 2s to 5s
description: Both PluginStatusManager (GET /plugins) and DaemonHealthMonitor (GET /health) polled independently every 2s, so up to 2 requests/second forever even at steady state. User-triggered changes already trigger an immediate refresh() outside the timer, so the ambient poll only needs to catch background state changes -- no reason to poll that aggressively.
close_reason: Bumped both poll intervals from 2000ms to 5000ms (DzPythonBridgePane's kPluginPollIntervalMs/kHealthPollIntervalMs, and matching class-level start() defaults). Verified build clean against SDK4/SDK6/standalone; pytest suite unaffected.

title: DzPythonBridge SDK4 build: DazPythonBridgeCore classes unlinked
description: Pre-existing gap found while working sop.2: DzPythonBridgePane.cpp uses AuthenticationService, PluginStatusManager, and now InlineRunner, all defined only in the DazPythonBridgeCore static lib. That lib is only linked into the DzPythonBridge SDK plugin target for DAZ_SDK_VERSION=6 (src/CMakeLists.txt) because DazPythonBridgeCore is built against plain Qt6 while SDK4 links Qt 4.8 -- mixing the two isn't ABI-safe. So a BUILD_DSS_PLUGIN=ON / DAZ_SDK_VERSION=4 build currently fails to link with unresolved externals for those classes. Needs a real fix: either building an SDK4-flavored copy of the Core classes, or restructuring so the DSS-facing pane code doesn't depend on Qt6-linked helpers under SDK4.
close_reason: Real SDK4 buildability, not just a link fix. Ported JsonStd.h (QVariant-based JSON, QJsonDocument on SDK6 / QtScript JS-eval fallback on SDK4) from daz-script-server; rewrote every QJson* call site in DazPythonBridgeCore across 5 files. Rewrote 25 new-style/lambda connect() calls to old-style SIGNAL()/SLOT() macros across 8 files (5 lambda QNetworkReply handlers became real slots using sender()/dynamic properties). Ported ~8 more Qt5-only APIs found only by actually building against the real SDK4 toolchain (QStringLiteral, QFileInfo::exists static, QProcess new-style API, QDir::removeRecursively, QRegularExpression, QStandardPaths, QUuid::WithoutBraces, a few QPlainTextEdit/QFontMetrics calls, plus a Q_ENUM->Q_ENUMS and QStringList init-list fix). Verified against real DAZ Studio SDK4 (Qt 4.8.1, moc.exe) and SDK6 devkits found on this machine: both DzPythonBridge.dll targets now build clean from a fresh configure. All 4 C++ harnesses and 49 pytest tests still pass.

title: DPB: reinstall/versioning semantics for plugin drops
description: Define what happens when a dropped zip manifest id matches an already-installed plugin -- should behave like the existing script registry convention, name is ID and overwrites existing, not silently fail or require a version-bump prompt.
design: On id match: stop the plugin running warm worker first, necessary on Windows since a live python.exe holds file locks on its own venv, extract and validate via the staged-install flow, atomically swap into the plugin dir, re-run eager dependency resolution so status cycles resolving to ready again, and log the version transition explicitly, matching the existing DSS log line format. Worker respawn stays lazy -- next invocation after reinstall spawns fresh with the new code.
acceptance_criteria: Dropping a zip with an existing plugin id replaces it cleanly with no stale worker holding old code or file locks, and the version change is visible in the log.
close_reason: ZipInstaller split into extractToStaging()/commit() so PluginInstaller can overwrite-by-id: stops the target's warm worker (best-effort HTTP POST /stop) before the file swap, swaps old/new dirs atomically, re-runs dependency resolution (fixed a real uv venv --clear bug found via testing), and emits reinstalled(id, oldVersion, newVersion). Covered by 3 new/updated manual C++ harnesses, all passing. Drag-and-drop UI to actually trigger/log this in DSS was never built by sop.3/.4 either -- still open, not unique to this issue.
